Passport Annexures - Complete Guide

What are Annexures? Annexures are self-declaration or verification forms required alongside the passport application in specific situations.

cation forms required alongside the passport application in specific situations. ANNEXURE A - Declaration of Emergency Contact and Identity
- Purpose: Provides emergency contact details and a self-declaration of identity
- Required by: All applicants (part of online application form now)

ANNEXURE B - Verification Certificate (Government Servants)
- Purpose: Identity and Address Verification Certificate for Government servants
- Required by: Government employees applying for a passport
- Who signs: Head of Office / authorized signatory

ANNEXURE C - No Objection Certificate for Government Servants
- Purpose: NOC from employer for government servants to obtain passport
- Required by: Government employees, including Central/State/PSU/Armed Forces
- Note: Without Annexure C, government employees cannot be issued a passport

ANNEXURE D - Declaration of Parent/Guardian for Minor Passport
- Purpose: Declaration by the applying parent AND consent of the other parent
- Required by: When only one parent is present at PSK for a minor's passport
- Must be notarized or sworn before a first-class magistrate

ANNEXURE E - Declaration for Change in Appearance
- Purpose: Self-declaration when there is a significant change in appearance
- Required when re-issuing a passport after surgery, major weight change, aging

ANNEXURE F - Verification Certificate by a Known Person for Tatkal
- Purpose: Identity verification certificate for Tatkal passport applications
- Acceptable signatories: Gazetted officer, MLA/MP, bank manager, doctor, lawyer, CA

ANNEXURE G - Declaration for Passport of a Child Born Out of Wedlock
- Purpose: Self-declaration for passport of a child born out of wedlock
- Must be sworn before a first-class magistrate or notary

ANNEXURE H - Non-Availability of Consent of the Other Parent
- Purpose: Declaration when consent of one parent is not available for minor's passport
- Common situations: Separated/divorced parents, single-parent families

ANNEXURE I - Declaration for Lost Passport
- Purpose: Self-declaration for passport lost or stolen
- Must be accompanied by FIR from local police station
- Lost passport fee is higher than normal re-issue fee

ANNEXURE J - Declaration for Damaged Passport
- Purpose: Self-declaration for a passport that has been physically damaged
- Must be accompanied by the damaged passport

IMPORTANT NOTES
- All annexures available for download at passportindia.gov.in
- Most annexures must be on non-judicial stamp paper (Rs.

t passportindia.gov.in
- Most annexures must be on non-judicial stamp paper (Rs. 10 or Rs. 20)
- Notarization is required for Annexure D, G, H, I, and J
- Carry 2 copies of each annexure to the PSK

Q: What documents are needed for a fresh passport? A: For a fresh passport you need: Aadhaar card (proof of identity + address), birth certificate or school certificate (proof of date of birth), and 2 recent passport-size photographs (white background, 2x2 inches).
Q: Tatkal passport kitne din mein milta hai? A: Tatkal passport generally 1 to 3 working days mein process hota hai police verification ke baad. Emergency cases mein same day bhi possible hai.
Q: What is the fee for a 36-page passport? A: The fee for a 36-page fresh passport (Normal) is Rs. 1500. For Tatkal, an additional fee of Rs. 2000 is charged, making it Rs. 3500 total.

Q: How long does it take to get a normal passport? A: A normal passport application takes approximately 30 to 45 working days from the date of application at the PSK, including police verification time.

Q: Can I apply for passport online? A: Yes, you must apply online through passportindia.gov.in. Register an account, fill the application form, pay the fee online, and then book an appointment at your nearest PSK.

Q: What is the minimum age to apply for a passport? A: There is no minimum age requirement for an Indian passport. Parents or legal guardians can apply on behalf of minor children, including newborns.

Q: What is police verification for passport? A: Police verification is mandatory for all passport applications. Local police verify your address and identity. For normal passports, it happens after issuance. For Tatkal, it may happen before issuance.

Q: How to track passport application status? A: Visit passportindia.gov.in and click Track Application Status. Enter your file number. You can also call 1800-258-1800 or SMS STATUS <file number> to 9704100100.

Q: What is Annexure D? A: Annexure D is a declaration by the applying parent and consent of the other parent, required when only one parent is present at PSK for a minor's passport application. It must be notarized.

Q: What are the photo specifications for passport? A: Passport photo must be 2x2 inches (51x51mm), white background, front-facing, full face, open eyes, neutral expression, no glasses, ears visible. Must be recent (within 3 months).

Q: How to renew an expired passport? A: Apply for Re-issue of Passport on passportindia.gov.in. Submit old passport details, pay the fee (Rs. 1500 for 36 pages), book appointment at PSK, and carry old passport original.
Q: What is the fee for a 60-page passport? A: The fee for a 60-page passport is Rs. 2000 for normal processing. For Tatkal, it is Rs. 2000 + Rs. 2000 = Rs. 4000 total.

Q: Can NRI apply for passport renewal? A: Yes, NRIs can apply for passport renewal at the nearest Indian Embassy or Consulate. They can also apply at a PSK during visits to India. Processing time abroad is typically 4-6 weeks.

Q: What documents does a minor need for passport? A: For a minor's passport: birth certificate (mandatory), Aadhaar card of child (if available), Aadhaar of both parents, consent of both parents, and school bonafide certificate.

Q: Passport ke liye kya kya documents chahiye? A: Passport ke liye Aadhaar card (identity + address proof), birth certificate ya school certificate (date of birth proof), aur 2 passport size photos chahiye. Tatkal ke liye additional verification document bhi chahiye.

Q: How to book appointment at PSK? A: After submitting online application and paying fee on passportindia.gov.in, go to Schedule Appointment, select your preferred PSK, choose available date and time slot, and confirm.

Q: What is the validity of Indian passport? A: For adults (18+): 10 years from date of issue. For minors (below 18): 5 years from date of issue OR until the minor reaches 18 years, whichever is earlier.

Q: Tatkal passport ke liye kitna paisa lagta hai? A: Tatkal passport ke liye normal fee ke upar Rs. 2000 extra lagta hai. 36 page booklet ke liye total Rs. 3500 (Rs. 1500 normal + Rs. 2000 Tatkal) hota hai.

Q: What happens if I lose my passport? A: If you lose your passport: 1) File an FIR at local police station, 2) Apply for re-issue on passportindia.gov.in selecting 'Lost Passport', 3) Submit FIR copy + Annexure I at PSK. Fee is Rs. 3000.

Q: Can I apply for passport from any city? A: Yes, under the All India Jurisdiction scheme, you can apply at any PSK regardless of your present address. Your application will be processed by the RPO covering your address.

Q: What is Annexure H? A: Annexure H is a declaration of non-availability of consent of the other parent, required for minor's passport when one parent is absent, missing, or refuses consent. Must be notarized.

Q: How to change name on passport after marriage? A: Apply for Re-issue of Passport on passportindia.gov.in. Select reason as 'Change in existing personal particulars'. Submit marriage certificate and Gazette notification (if applicable) at PSK.

Q: What is the difference between PSK and RPO? A: PSK (Passport Seva Kendra) is a front-end office managed by TCS that handles applications, documents, and biometrics. RPO (Regional Passport Office) is a government office that handles granting/approval of passports.

Q: Is Aadhaar card mandatory for passport? A: Aadhaar is not mandatory but is the most preferred and widely accepted document. It serves as both identity proof and address proof. You can use alternative documents from the approved list.

Q: What is PCC (Police Clearance Certificate)? A: PCC is a certificate issued by Indian police authorities stating that the applicant has no criminal record. It costs Rs. 500 and is required by some countries for immigration or visa purposes.

Q: Bacche ka passport kaise banaye? A: Bacche ka passport banane ke liye: 1) passportindia.gov.in par apply karein, 2) Birth certificate, dono parents ka Aadhaar, 3) Dono parents ki consent chahiye, 4) PSK mein appointment lein.

Q: Can I get passport without birth certificate? A: For applicants born on or after 26.01.1989, birth certificate from Registrar of Births & Deaths is MANDATORY. For those born before this date, school leaving/matriculation certificate is accepted.

Q: What are the types of Indian passport? A: Three types: 1) Ordinary Passport (Type P) - for citizens, 36 or 60 pages, 2) Official Passport (Type S) - for government officials on duty, 3) Diplomatic Passport (Type D) - for diplomats.

Q: How to apply for Tatkal passport? A: Apply online at passportindia.gov.in, select Tatkal scheme, pay normal fee + Rs. 2000 Tatkal surcharge, book a Tatkal appointment at PSK, carry additional verification document (Aadhaar/PAN/Voter ID).

Q: Passport ka status kaise check karein? A: passportindia.gov.in par jaake Track Application Status par click karein. File number daalen. Ya phir 1800-258-1800 par call karein. SMS bhi bhej sakte hain: STATUS <file number> to 9704100100.
Q: What is the helpline number for passport? A: The toll-free helpline is 1800-258-1800, operating Monday to Saturday, 8:00 AM to 10:00 PM. For NRI queries: +91-11-40504050.
Q: Can I get passport in one day? A: In genuine emergency cases under Tatkal scheme, same-day processing may be possible. However, typically Tatkal passports take 1-3 working days after PSK visit.
Q: What is the fee for minor passport? A: Fresh passport for minors (36 pages) costs Rs. 1000 under normal scheme. Under Tatkal, it is Rs. 1000 + Rs. 2000 = Rs. 3000 total.

Q: Do I need to carry original documents to PSK? A: Yes, you must carry ALL original documents to the PSK for verification, along with one set of self-attested photocopies. DigiLocker documents are also accepted.
Q: What if my passport is damaged? A: Apply for Re-issue on passportindia.gov.in selecting 'Damaged Passport'. Submit the damaged passport along with Annexure J and an explanation letter at PSK.

Q: Kya government servant ko NOC chahiye? A: Haan, government servants ko Annexure B (Verification Certificate) aur Annexure C (NOC) apne employer se lena zaruri hai. Bina NOC ke passport issue nahi hoga.

Q: How many pages can I choose for passport? A: You can choose either a 36-page or 60-page passport booklet. The 60-page booklet is recommended for frequent travelers. 36 pages costs Rs. 1500 and 60 pages costs Rs. 2000.
Q: What is POPSK? A: POPSK stands for Post Office Passport Seva Kendra. These are passport service centers set up at select post offices for the convenience of applicants in smaller cities and towns.

Q: Can divorced parent apply for child passport? A: Yes, the parent with legal custody can apply. Court order granting custody must be submitted. Annexure D or Annexure H is required depending on whether other parent's consent is available.

Q: What is DigiLocker and can I use it for passport? A: DigiLocker is a government digital document storage platform. Yes, DigiLocker documents are accepted at all PSKs — you don't need to carry physical copies of documents stored in DigiLocker.
Q: PSK mein kitna time lagta hai? A: PSK mein poora process lagbhag 2-3 ghante ka hota hai. Counter A: Token, Counter B: Document check, Counter C: Biometric, Counter D: Final verification.

Q: Can I reschedule my PSK appointment? A: Yes, you can reschedule up to 3 times. Log in to passportindia.gov.in → View Saved/Submitted Applications → Schedule Appointment → Reschedule. Applications expire after 3 months of payment.
Q: What is Annexure F? A: Annexure F is a verification certificate for Tatkal passport applications, signed by a known authority like a gazetted officer, MLA/MP, bank manager, doctor, lawyer, or CA.

Q: Passport ke liye online payment kaise karein? A: Online payment Credit Card, Debit Card, Net Banking, ya UPI se kar sakte hain. SBI Bank Challan bhi available hai. PSK par cash payment accepted nahi hai. Fee refundable nahi hai.

Q: Is walk-in allowed at PSK? A: Walk-in appointments are not available at all PSKs. Some RPOs allow walk-in for senior citizens (65+), government officials, and physically challenged individuals. Check with your local PSK.

Q: What if police verification is adverse? A: If police verification report is adverse, the passport may be put on hold or the application may be rejected. For Tatkal passports, an adverse report may lead to passport impoundment. You can appeal through the RPO.
Q: Can I convert normal application to Tatkal? A: No, you cannot convert a normal application to Tatkal after submission. You must submit a new application with Tatkal fee selected.
Q: Passport kitne saal ka hota hai? A: Adults (18 saal se upar) ke liye passport 10 saal ke liye valid hota hai. Minors (18 se kam) ke liye 5 saal ya 18 saal ki umar tak, jo pehle ho.

Q: What is ECR and ECNR category? A: ECR (Emigration Check Required) means you need emigration clearance for traveling to certain countries for work. ECNR (Emigration Check Not Required) is for educated individuals (10th pass or above). ECNR is printed on the passport.

Q: What is the address proof for passport? A: Accepted address proofs include: Aadhaar card, Voter ID, bank passbook/statement, utility bills (water/electricity/gas/telephone - not older than 3 months), income tax order, or registered rent agreement.

Q: How is passport delivered after processing? A: Passport is printed at India Security Press and dispatched via Speed Post to your registered address. You can track the dispatch status and Speed Post tracking number on passportindia.gov.in.

Q: Naya passport banane mein kitna kharcha aata hai? A: Naya 36-page passport ka normal fee Rs. 1500 hai. 60-page passport ka Rs. 2000 hai. Minor (18 se kam) ke liye Rs. 1000 hai. Tatkal mein Rs. 2000 extra lagta hai.

Q: Can I apply for passport without Aadhaar? A: Yes, Aadhaar is not mandatory. You can use alternative documents like Voter ID, PAN card, driving license for identity proof, and utility bills or bank statements for address proof.

Q: What is the grievance redressal mechanism? A: You can file grievances through: 1) Passport Seva helpline 1800-258-1800, 2) Grievance portal on passportindia.gov.in, 3) Write to the CPV division of MEA, 4) CPGRAMS portal for government grievances.

Q: Passport gum ho gaya toh kya karein? A: 1) Police station mein FIR darj karein, 2) passportindia.gov.in par re-issue apply karein (Lost Passport select karein), 3) FIR copy + Annexure I PSK mein jama karein. Fee Rs. 3000 hai.

Q: What documents are needed for name change on passport? A: For name change: marriage certificate (if after marriage), Gazette notification of name change, deed poll, and affidavit. Apply for re-issue with reason 'Change in personal particulars'.

Q: Is there any age limit for passport? A: There is no minimum or maximum age limit for an Indian passport. Newborn babies can get passports, and senior citizens can apply or renew passports at any age.

Q: What is Annexure I? A: Annexure I is a self-declaration required when applying for re-issue of a lost passport. It must be accompanied by an FIR from the local police station with details of the lost passport.

2. Voter ID Card (Election Photo Identity Card / EPIC)
3. PAN Card
4. Driving License

6. Bank/Post Office passbook with photo

Deaths or the Municipal Corporation is MANDATORY (Government Order dated 2018). School leaving certificate alone is NOT sufficient for this age group. SECTION 4: DOCUMENTS FOR MINOR PASSPORT
1. Birth Certificate (MANDATORY for all minors)
2. Aadhaar Card of the child (if available)
3. Aadhaar Card of both parents

5. School bonafide certificate / ID card

 - If both parents are present at PSK: both sign the application

SECTION 5: DOCUMENTS FOR RE-ISSUE
1.

opted children: legal adoption certificate

SECTION 5: DOCUMENTS FOR RE-ISSUE
1. Old passport (original + self-attested photocopy of first 2 and last 2 pages)
2. If passport is lost: FIR copy from local police station + Annexure I
3. If passport is damaged: damaged passport + explanation letter
4. For name change: Gazette notification / marriage certificate / deed poll
5.

For name change: Gazette notification / marriage certificate / deed poll
5. For address change: new address proof document

SECTION 6: DOCUMENTS FOR GOVERNMENT SERVANTS
1. Identity Certificate / No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the employer
2. Prior political clearance from MEA (for diplomatic/official passports)
3.

Prior political clearance from MEA (for diplomatic/official passports)
3. Forwarding letter from the ministry / department

SECTION 7: PHOTOGRAPH SPECIFICATIONS
- Size: 2 inches x 2 inches (51 mm x 51 mm)
- Background: White or off-white (plain, no patterns)
- Pose: Front-facing, full face, open eyes
- Expression: Neutral, mouth closed
- Head covering: Not allowed unless for religious reasons
- Glasses: Remove glasses for the photo
- The photo should be recent (taken within last 3 months)
- Print quality must be high (no pixelation, proper contrast)
- Ears should be visible
- For infants: photo of child alone (no parent holding)

SECTION 8: GENERAL RULES
- All original documents must be presented at PSK for verification
- Self-attested photocopies (1 set) must be submitted at PSK
- Documents in languages other than Hindi/English must be accompanied by a notarized translation
- Laminated documents must be accompanied by an unlaminated copy
- DigiLocker documents are accepted at all PSKs (no physical copy needed)

PAYMENT METHODS
- Online payment via Credit Card, Debit Card, Net Banking, or UPI
- SBI Bank Challan payment (offline option)

FEE EXEMPTIONS

- Senior citizens pay the same fee as other adults
- Government servants pay the same fee (department may reimburse)

ADDITIONAL CHARGES (NOT included in passport fee)
- Speed Post charges for passport delivery: included in the fee

IMPORTANT NOTES

- GST is NOT applicable on passport fees (government service)
- Fee for Tatkal is the same regardless of the reason for urgency

Passport Seva Portal - Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

1. What is a Passport? A passport is a travel document, usually issued by a country's government to its citizens, that certifies the identity and nationality of its holder primarily for the purpose of international travel. Indian passports are issued by the Ministry of External Affairs (MEA), Government of India. 2.

2. What are the types of Passport issued in India? The following types of passports are issued in India:
- Ordinary Passport (Type P) – Issued to ordinary citizens for personal travel, with 36 or 60 pages. - Official Passport (Type S) – Issued to individuals representing the Government of India on official business.

Issued to individuals representing the Government of India on official business. - Diplomatic Passport (Type D) – Issued to Indian diplomats and top-ranking government officials. 3. What is the validity of an Indian Passport? - For adults (18 years and above): Valid for 10 years from the date of issue.

t? - For adults (18 years and above): Valid for 10 years from the date of issue. - For minors (below 18 years): Valid for 5 years from the date of issue OR until the minor reaches 18 years of age, whichever is earlier. 4. How to apply for a Passport? Step 1: Register on the Passport Seva Portal at passportindia.gov.in. Step 2: Log in and fill out the online application form.

Step 2: Log in and fill out the online application form. Select "Fresh Passport" or "Re-issue of Passport" as applicable. Step 3: Pay the applicable fee online via credit/debit card, net banking, or SBI challan. Step 4: Schedule an appointment at your nearest Passport Seva Kendra (PSK) or Regional Passport Office (RPO).

nt at your nearest Passport Seva Kendra (PSK) or Regional Passport Office (RPO). Step 5: Visit the PSK/RPO on the scheduled date with all original documents and the printed application receipt. Step 6: After verification, your passport will be dispatched to your address. 5. What is the difference between a PSK and an RPO?

5. What is the difference between a PSK and an RPO? - PSK (Passport Seva Kendra): Front-end offices managed by TCS in partnership with MEA. They handle passport applications, document verification, and biometric capture. - RPO (Regional Passport Office): Government offices that handle granting (approval) of passports. Complex cases are referred here.

Complex cases are referred here. - POPSK (Post Office Passport Seva Kendra): Passport services available at select post offices for convenience. 6. What is the online portal for passport services? The official portal is passportindia.gov.in. All passport applications must be submitted online through this portal. You can also track application status, schedule appointments, and download forms. 7.

7. Can I apply for a passport from anywhere in India? Yes. Passport services are available under the "All India Jurisdiction" scheme. You can apply at any PSK regardless of your present address. Your application will be processed by the RPO under whose jurisdiction your present address falls. 8. What is police verification? Police verification is mandatory for all passport applications.

ce verification? Police verification is mandatory for all passport applications. After you submit your application at the PSK, the local police will verify your address and identity. For fresh passports, police verification typically happens AFTER passport issuance. For Tatkal passports, police verification is done BEFORE passport issuance in most cases. 9. How long does it take to get a passport?

9. How long does it take to get a passport? - Normal Application: 30 to 45 working days from the date of application. - Tatkal Application: 1 to 3 working days after submission at PSK. - Police verification can add additional time depending on your local police station's workload. 10. How can I track my passport application status?

10. How can I track my passport application status? You can track your application status by:
- Visiting passportindia.gov.in and clicking on "Track Application Status"
- Entering your file number (starts with your RPO code)
- Calling the toll-free helpline: 1800-258-1800
- Sending an SMS: STATUS <file number> to 9704100100

11. What is the helpline number for passport services?

What is the helpline number for passport services? The toll-free helpline number is 1800-258-1800. It operates Monday to Saturday, 8:00 AM to 10:00 PM. You can also reach the National Call Centre for passport queries. 12. Can I download my passport online? No. The original passport booklet is printed and dispatched by India Security Press. You cannot download a digital copy of the passport.
You cannot download a digital copy of the passport. However, you can check the dispatch status and speed post tracking number online.

NRI Passport Guidelines - Special Rules for Non-Resident Indians

Who is an NRI? A Non-Resident Indian (NRI) is an Indian citizen who resides outside India for employment, business, education, or any other purpose. NRIs hold Indian passports and are Indian nationals. Passport Services for NRIs
NRIs can avail passport services through:
1. Indian Missions/Embassies/Consulates abroad
2.

Indian Missions/Embassies/Consulates abroad
2. Passport Seva Kendras in India (during visits to India)

Applying for Passport Abroad (Through Indian Mission)
- Visit the website of the nearest Indian Embassy/Consulate
- Download and fill the passport application form
- Submit at the Indian Mission with required documents
- Processing time varies by mission (typically 4-6 weeks)
- Fee is collected in local currency equivalent

Documents Required for NRIs
1.

- Fee is collected in local currency equivalent

Documents Required for NRIs
1. Current passport (original + copies)
2. Valid visa / residence permit of the country of residence
3. Proof of Indian citizenship (if first-time applicant)
4. Address proof in India (permanent address for passport records)
5. Address proof abroad (utility bill, bank statement, etc.)
6.

Address proof abroad (utility bill, bank statement, etc.)
6. Photographs as per Indian passport specifications
7.

Photographs as per Indian passport specifications
7. For minors: birth certificate + parents' passports

Re-issue of Passport for NRIs
- Can be done at the nearest Indian Mission
- Old passport must be surrendered
- For lost passports abroad: file a police report in the country of residence + report to Indian Mission
- Emergency Certificate can be issued for immediate travel back to India

Special Rules
- NRIs can use both Indian and foreign address on passport application
- Police verification is done at the permanent address in India
- Tatkal service is generally NOT available at Indian Missions abroad
- Processing time at missions is longer than in India
- OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) card holders are NOT Indian citizens and cannot hold Indian passports

NRI Helpline
- Ministry of External Affairs NRI helpline: +91-11-40504050
- MADAD portal for NRI grievances: madad.gov.in
- Email: passport@mea.gov.in

Passport Seva - Complete Application Guide

PART 1: FRESH PASSPORT APPLICATION

1.1 Who Can Apply for a Fresh Passport? Any Indian citizen can apply for a fresh passport. There is no minimum age requirement. Parents or legal guardians apply on behalf of minor children. 1.2 Online Application Process
Step 1: Visit passportindia.gov.in and click "New User?

line Application Process
Step 1: Visit passportindia.gov.in and click "New User? Register Now"
Step 2: Create an account using your email ID. Verify your email.

Verify your email. Step 3: Log in and click "Apply for Fresh Passport / Re-issue of Passport"
Step 4: Fill in the application form carefully:
 - Personal Details (name, date of birth, place of birth, gender)
 - Family Details (father's name, mother's name, spouse name if applicable)
 - Present Address (must match your address proof document)
 - Emergency Contact Details
 - Identity & Address Proof Document Details
 - Passport Details (if re-issuing)
Step 5: Self-certify the application
Step 6: Pay the fee online
Step 7: Schedule an appointment at your preferred PSK
Step 8: Print the Application Receipt (ARN - Application Reference Number)

ation Receipt (ARN - Application Reference Number)

1.3 What Happens at the PSK? When you visit the PSK on your appointment date:
Counter A: Token generation and initial document check
Counter B: Detailed scrutiny of original documents and photocopies
Counter C: Biometric capture (photograph, fingerprints, signature)
Counter D: Final verification by the Passport Granting Officer

The entire process at the PSK takes approximately 2-3 hours.

- For Normal applications: Passport is printed and dispatched after police verification (if no adverse report). - Police verification is conducted by your local police station. - Passport is dispatched via Speed Post to your registered address.

- Passport is dispatched via Speed Post to your registered address. - You can track dispatch status on passportindia.gov.in. PART 2: RE-ISSUE OF PASSPORT

2.1 When Do You Need to Re-issue?

PART 2: RE-ISSUE OF PASSPORT

2.1 When Do You Need to Re-issue? - Passport validity expired or expiring within 1 year
- Pages exhausted (no more blank visa pages)
- Change in personal details (name, address, spouse name, etc.)
- Lost or damaged passport
- Change from minor to adult passport (on turning 18)

2.2 Re-issue Application Process
The process is similar to fresh passport application.

-issue Application Process
The process is similar to fresh passport application. Key differences:
- Select "Re-issue of Passport" instead of "Fresh Passport"
- You must provide old passport details (number, date of issue, place of issue)
- Submit the original old passport at PSK
- For lost passports: file an FIR first, then submit the FIR copy with your application

2.3 Short Validity Passport
If your passport has less than 6 months validity, many countries will not grant you a visa.

sport has less than 6 months validity, many countries will not grant you a visa. It is advisable to apply for re-issue when your passport has 1 year or less validity remaining. PART 3: TATKAL (URGENT) PASSPORT

et a passport on an urgent basis, typically within 1-3 working days of applying. It requires additional fees and additional documentation. 3.2 Tatkal Eligibility

3.2 Tatkal Eligibility
Any Indian citizen can apply under Tatkal scheme. You must provide one of the following additional documents as verification:
- Aadhaar card
- Voter ID
- PAN card
- Verification certificate from an authority (Annexure F)
- Any document from the "Proof of Identity" list that has been verified by the issuing authority

2,000 additional
These are IN ADDITION to the normal passport fees. 3.4 Tatkal Processing
- Application is processed on priority basis at the PSK
- Police verification may be done BEFORE passport issuance for Tatkal cases
- Passport is typically dispatched within 1-3 working days
- Post-issuance police verification is also conducted

PART 4: MINOR PASSPORT

4.1 Rules for Minor Passport (Below 18 years)
- Both parents must give consent (signatures on the application)
- If only one parent is applying, an Annexure D or Annexure H may be required
- Minor's birth certificate is mandatory
- School leaving certificate / bonafide certificate may serve as additional proof
- Validity: 5 years or until the minor turns 18, whichever is earlier
- No last page address (since 2017 policy change)

4.2 Single Parent / Divorced Parent Cases
- If parents are divorced: court order granting custody must be submitted
- Annexure D: Declaration by the applying parent and consent of the other parent
- Annexure H: If other parent's consent cannot be obtained
- In case of child born out of wedlock: Annexure G may be required

Tatkal Passport - Complete Guide

What is Tatkal Passport? Tatkal passport is an urgent/expedited passport service offered by the Government of India for citizens who need a passport on an urgent basis. The word "Tatkal" means "immediate" in Hindi. Who Can Apply for Tatkal Passport? Any Indian citizen can apply for a Tatkal passport.
n Apply for Tatkal Passport? Any Indian citizen can apply for a Tatkal passport. It is available for both fresh passport applications and re-issue applications.

It is available for both fresh passport applications and re-issue applications. Processing Time for Tatkal Passport
- Tatkal applications are processed on priority at the Passport Seva Kendra
- Typical processing time: 1 to 3 working days after submitting application at PSK
- In genuine emergency cases, same-day processing may be available

Tatkal Fees
The Tatkal fee is charged IN ADDITION to the normal passport fee:
- 36-page booklet: Rs.

al fee is charged IN ADDITION to the normal passport fee:
- 36-page booklet: Rs. 1,500 (normal) + Rs. 2,000 (Tatkal surcharge) = Rs. 3,500 total
- 60-page booklet: Rs. 2,000 (normal) + Rs. 2,000 (Tatkal surcharge) = Rs. 4,000 total
- Minor (below 18): Rs. 1,000 (normal) + Rs. 2,000 (Tatkal surcharge) = Rs.

1,000 (normal) + Rs. 2,000 (Tatkal surcharge) = Rs. 3,000 total

Documents Required for Tatkal Passport (Additional)
In addition to normal passport documents, Tatkal applicants must provide ONE of the following:
1. Aadhaar Card
2. Voter ID Card (EPIC)
3. PAN Card
4. Verification Certificate from an employer of a reputed company (Annexure F)
5.

Verification Certificate from an employer of a reputed company (Annexure F)
5. Verification Certificate from an educational institution (for students)

Police Verification for Tatkal
- For Tatkal passports, police verification is typically initiated BEFORE passport issuance
- Pre-verification police report is obtained before printing the passport
- Additional post-issuance police verification may also be conducted
- If police report is adverse, the passport may be impounded

Tatkal Passport for Minors
- Same Tatkal surcharge applies for minors
- Both parents' consent is required (or Annexure D/H)
- Birth certificate is mandatory
- School bonafide certificate may be required

Emergency Certificate vs Tatkal
- Emergency Certificate: Issued by Indian missions abroad for emergency travel when regular passport is unavailable
- Tatkal Passport: Urgent passport issued within India through the regular passport system
- Emergency certificates are single-journey documents, not full passports

Important Notes
- Tatkal scheme is available at all PSKs across India
- You must book a Tatkal appointment specifically (not a normal appointment)
- There is no way to convert a normal application to Tatkal after submission
- If you need to change to Tatkal, you must submit a new application with Tatkal fee
- Tatkal passport has the same validity as normal passport (10 years for adults, 5 years for minors)

6. Refusal of passports, travel documents. etc. (1) Subject to the other provisions of this Act, the passport authority shall refuse 
to make an endorsement for visiting any foreign country under clause (b) or clause 
(c) of sub-section (2) of section 5 on any one or more of the following grounds, and 
no other ground, namely: - 
 (a) that the applicant may, or is likely to, engage in such country in activities 
prejudicial to the sovereignty and integrity of India: 
 (b) that the presence of the applicant in such country may, or is likely to, be 
detrimental to the security of India; 
 (c) that the presence of the applicant in such country may, or is likely to, 
prejudice the friendly relations of India with that or any other country, 
 (d) that in the opinion of the Central Government the presence of the applicant 
in such country is not in the public interest.

nt the presence of the applicant 
in such country is not in the public interest. (2) Subject to the other provisions of this Act, the passport authority shall refuse 
to issue a passport or travel document for visiting any foreign country under clause

(c) of sub-section (2) of section 5 on any one or more of the following grounds, and 
on no other ground, namely: - 
 (a) that the applicant is not a citizen of India., 
 (b) that the applicant may, or is likely to, engage outside India in activities 
prejudicial to the sovereignty and integrity of India., 
 (c) that the departure of the applicant from India may, or is likely to, be 
detrimental to the security of India; 
 (d) that the presence of the applicant outside India may, or is likely to, 
prejudice the friendly relations of India with any foreign country; 
 (e) that the applicant has, at any time during the period of five years 
immediately preceding the date of his application, been convicted by a court in India 
for any offence involving moral turpitude and sentenced in respect thereof to 
imprisonment for not less than two years; 
 (f) that proceedings in respect of an offence alleged to have been committed by 
the applicant are pending before a criminal court in India; 
 (g) that a warrant or summons for the appearance, or a warrant for the arrest, 
of the applicant has been issued by a court under any law for the time being in force 
or that an order prohibiting the departure from India of the applicant has been made 
by any such court; 
 (h) that the applicant has been repatriated and has not reimbursed the 
expenditure incurred in connection with such repatriation; 
 (i) that in the opinion of the Central Government the issue of a passport or 
travel document to the applicant will not be in the public interest.

15. Previous sanction of Central Government necessary 
No prosecution shall be instituted against any person in respect of any offence under 
this Act without the previous sanction of the Central Government or such officer or 
authority as may be authorized by that Government by order in writing in this behalf. 16.

16. Protection of action taken in good faith 
No suit, prosecution or other, legal proceeding shall lie against the Government or 
any officer or authority for anything which is in good faith done or intended to be 
done tinder this Act. 17.

A passport or travel document issued under this Act shall in all times remain the 
property of the Central Government. 13[***] 
19. Passports and travel documents to be invalid for travel to 
certain countries 
Upon the issue of t notification by the Central Government that a foreign country is-

(a) a country which is committing external aggression against India; or 
 (b) a country assisting the country committing external aggression against India; 
or 
 (c) a country where armed hostilities are in progress; or 
 (d) a country to which travel must be restricted in the public interest because 
such travel would seriously impair the conduct of foreign affairs of the Government 
of India, 
a passport or travel document for travel through or visiting such country shall cease 
to be valid for such travel or visit unless in any case a special endorsement in that 
behalf is made in the prescribed form by the prescribed authority.

20. Issue of passports and travel documents to persons who 
are not citizens of lndia 
Notwithstanding anything contained in the foregoing provisions relating to issue of a 
passport or travel document, the Central Government may issue, or cause to be 
issued, a passport or travel document to a person who is not a citizen of lndia if that 
Government is of the opinion that it is necessary so to do in the public interest.

21. Power to delegate 
The Central Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, direct that any 
power or function which may be exercised or performed by it under this Act other 
than the power under clause (d) of sub-section (1) of section 6 or the power under 
clause (i) of sub-section (2) of that section or the power under section 24, may, in 
relation to such matters and subject to such conditions, if any, as it may specify in 
the notification, be exercised or performed- 
 (a) by such officer or authority subordinate to the Central Government; or 
 (b) by any State Government or by any officer or authority subordinate to such 
Government; or 
 (c) in any foreign country in which there is no diplomatic mission of India, by such 
Consular Officer; 
as may be specified in the notification.

22. Power to exempt 
Where the Central Government is of the opinion that it is necessary or expedient in 
the public interest so to do, it may, by notification in the Official Gazette and subject 
to such conditions, if any, as it may specify in the notification,- 
 (a) exempt any person or class of persons from the operation of all or any of the 
provisions of this Act or the rules made thereunder; and

(b) as often as may be, cancel any such notification and again subject, by a like 
notification, the person or class of persons to the operation of such provisions. 23.

23. Act to be in addition to certain enactments 
The provisions of this Act shall be in addition to and not in derogation of the 
provisions of the Passport (Entry into India) Act, 1920 (34 of 1920), the 
14[Emigration Act, 1983 (31 of 1983), the Registration of Foreigners Act, 1939 (16 of 
1939), the Foreigners Act, 1946 (31 of 1946), 15[***] the Trading with the Ememy 
(Continuance of Emergency Provisions) Act, 1947 (16 of 1947), the Foreigners Law 
(Application and Amendment) Act, 1962 (42 of 1962), 16[the Foreign Exchange 
Regulation Act, 1973] and other enactments relating to foreigners and foreign 
exchange.

on Act, 1973] and other enactments relating to foreigners and foreign 
exchange. 24. Power to make rules 
 (1) The Central Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette, make 
rules for carrying out the purposes of this Act.

in the Official Gazette, make 
rules for carrying out the purposes of this Act. (2) In particular, and without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing power, 
such rules may provide for all or any of the following matters, namely:-

respectively in sub-section (1) and sub-section (2) of section 4 may be issued; 
 (c) the form and particulars of application for the issue or renewal of a passport 
or travel document or for endorsement on a passport or travel document and where

 (d) the period for which passports and travel documents shall continue in force; 
 (e) the form in which and the conditions subject to which the different classes 
of passports and travel documents may be issued, renewed or varied;

11, the jurisdiction of, and the procedure which may be followed by, such appellate

(h) the services (including the issue of a duplicate passport or travel document

made, before each House of Parliament while it is in session for a total period of 
thirty days which may be comprised in one session or 19[in two or more successive 
sessions, and if, before the expiry of the session immediately following the session or 
the successive sessions aforesaid] in which it is so laid or the session immediately 
following, both Houses agree in making any modification in the rule or both Houses 
agree that the rule should not be made, the rule shall thereafter have effect only in 
such modified form or be of no effect, as the case may be; so however, that any 
such modification or annulment shall be without prejudice to the validity or anything 
previously done under that rule.

Act, 1967. Passport is a very valuable document.

to the Passport Office or Indian Mission, if abroad.

Note.­
1. 'NA' means 'not applicable'. 2. Fee for a passport under Tatkaal scheme is inclusive of normal passport fee. 3. Tatkaal fee will be charged for issue of fresh passport within 1-7 working days 
of the date of application. In case of reissue of passports, Tatkaal fee will be 
applicable for 1-3 working days.

e of reissue of passports, Tatkaal fee will be 
applicable for 1-3 working days. The prescribed time limit is subject to fulfillment 
of all documentary requirements and completion of requisite formalities. 4.

4. No Tatkaal fee is payable in the following cases, namely: 
(i) -an applicant who has been advised to go abroad for specialised medical 
treatment and consultation (proof required for fee exemption), and an 
attendant; 
(ii) death abroad of an applicant's spouse, father, mother, child, son-in-law, 
daughter-In-law, grandchild, brother or his spouse, sister or her spouse 
(applicant should provide sufficient details as to when and where death took 
place); 
(iii) all Diplomatic or Official passport holders (except persons, who were issued 
short validity diplomatic or official passports for short visits abroad) who apply 
for an ordinary passport in lieu of their Diplomatic or Official passport up to 
three years after expiry of passport; 
casual visitors from India, whose passports are stolen or lost or damaged while 
abroad; 
in Missions or Posts abroad, if a passport is issued in normal course on first 
come first served basis even within Tatkaal timeframe; 
(vi) inter-country adoption cases if passport is issued within Tatkaal period.]

CONDITIONS RELATING TO THE ISSUE OF PASSPORT AND 
TRAVEL DOCUMENT 
1.

rule 19) 
CONDITIONS RELATING TO THE ISSUE OF PASSPORT AND 
TRAVEL DOCUMENT 
1. A passport or travel document is available for travel only to the countries specified 
therein and must not be utilised for travel to other countries. 2. A passport or travel document must not be utilised for travel to countries not 
recognised by the Government of India. 3.

3. A passport or travel document should not be sent out of any country by post. 4. A passport or travel document should not be allowed to pass into the possession of 
any unauthorised person. 5. The holder of a passport or travel document is personally responsible for its safe 
custody. It must not be wilfully lost, damaged or destroyed.

It must not be wilfully lost, damaged or destroyed. In case of an 
unintentional loss or destruction, the fact and circumstances of such loss or 
destruction should be immediately reported to the nearest passport authority in India 
or (if the holder of the passport is abroad) to the nearest Indian Mission or Post and 
to the local police. 6.

6. A Passport or travel document must not be altered or mutilated in any way nor any 
endorsement made in it by any person other than a duly authorised official. 7. If the particulars of children are included in the passport or travel document of parent 
or guardian, as the case may be, the children shall not travel alone. Ii

8. Children, whose names are included in the passport or travel document of their 
parent or legal guardian, should apply for a separate passport on attaining the age 
of 15 years. 9.

9. When a citizen of India abroad is to be repatriated to India at the expense of 
G~lVernment of India, he shall surrender his passport or travel document to the 
Indian Mission or Post repatriating him and obtain an Emergency Certificate for 
direct return to India. 10. The holder of an Emergency Certificate, on arrival in India, shall surrender it to the 
Immigration Check Post. 11.

11. The passport or travel document should be surrendered to the passport authority 
if he ceases to be eligible to hold one. 12. A diplomatic or official passport shall automatic~.lIy cease to be valid if the person 
to whom it was issued ceases to exercise the functions which rendered him eligible 
to receive a diplomatic or official passport.

ANNEXURE 
 
SIMPLIFICATION OF PASSPORT RULES 
 
 In order to streamline, liberalize and ease the process of issue of passport, the 
Ministry of External Affairs has taken a number of steps in the realm of passport 
policy to ease the process of issue of pass ports. The details of these steps are given 
below:- 
 
A.

The details of these steps are given 
below:- 
 
A. Documents in support of proof of Date of Birth 
 
 As per the extant statutory provisions of the Passport Rules, 1980, all the 
applicants born on or after 26/01/1989, in order to get a passport, had to, hitherto, 
mandatorily submit the Birth Certificate as th e proof of Date of Birth (DOB).

ce Corporations/Companies 
having the DOB of the holder of the insurance policy. B. Other Changes: 
 
(i) The online passport application form now requires the applicant to provide 
the name of father or mother or legal guardian, i.e., only one parent and not 
both. This would enable single parents to apply for passports for their

children and to also issue passports where the name of either the father or the 
mother is not required to be printed at the request of the applicant. (ii) The total number of Annexes prescribed in the Passport Rule, 1980, has been 
reduced to 9 from the present 15. Annexes A, C, D, E, J, and K have been 
removed and certain Annexes have been merged.

es A, C, D, E, J, and K have been 
removed and certain Annexes have been merged. (iii) All the annexes that are required to be given by the applicants would be in 
the form of a self declaration on a plain paper. No attestation/swearing by/ 
before any Notary/ Executive Magistrate/ First Class Judicial Magistrate 
would be henceforth necessary.

tive Magistrate/ First Class Judicial Magistrate 
would be henceforth necessary. (iv) Married applicants would not be re quired to provide the erstwhile Annexure 
K or any marriage certificate. (v) The passport application form does not require the applicant to provide the 
name of her/his spouse in case of separated or divorced persons.

to provide the 
name of her/his spouse in case of separated or divorced persons. Such 
applicants for passports would not be required to provide even the Divorce 
Decree. (vi) In case of children not born out of wedlock, the applicant for the passport of 
such children should submit only extant Annexure C while submitting the 
passport application.

should submit only extant Annexure C while submitting the 
passport application. (vii) In case of issue of passport to in-country domestically adopted children, 
submission of the registered adoption deed would no longer be required. In 
the absence of any deed to this effect, the passport applicant may give a 
declaration on a plain paper confirming the adoption.

port applicant may give a

recorded in the passport. In case there is a discrepancy between the DOB

consider the explanation of each applicant seeking change in the DOB 
(irrespective of the period that would have lapsed after the issue of the 
passport) to find the genuineness of the claim and if the PIA is satisfied with 
the claim and with the document(s) submitted by the applicant in support of 
the claim, the PIA shall accept all such requests made by the applicant to 
issue the passport with revised DOB.
